California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
December 20′  1990
He1en M. Kanolrsky
city clerk
city of West Sacramento
P. O. Box 966
west sacramento, cA 95691
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-90-639
Dear Ms. Kanowsky 3
You have requested assistance regarding the-conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.r/ 
The city of
west Sacranento is planningr to update its conf l-ict of interest
code in the near future. 
You are asking if we could review the
code and suggest any changes or revj-sions $/e may be necessary.
Since your iequest is for general assistance, we are treating it
as a request for informal assistance.zl
You should understand that since we have no knowledge of the
duties and respons ibi t ities of the persons designated, we cannot
determine if the assigned discl,osure is appropriate or that all
persons within the city !'rho are in dec is ion-maki'ng capaciti'es are
covered by the code.
I have itemized the areas of concern or needed revisions in
the order of thej-r appearance in the code.
1. The Resolution appropriately incorporates the provisions
of Commission Regulation 1,8730 as the standard terms of the code'
The reference to that regulation, 2 California Adninistrative code
Sectj-on :.a73o, was recentLy changed to 2 california Code of
Regulations Section 18730. That reference needs to be corrected
in the second paragraph of the resolution.
l/ 
Governnent code Sections 81ooo-91015. Al'L statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwi-se indicated.
commission reguLations appear at 2 california code of ReguLations
Section 18OOO, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations'
2/ Infornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or forrnal written advice'
lcovernineirt code Seation grrLc i 2 cal-. code of Regs' section
1832e (c) (3) . )
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 0 Sacramento CA 95804‐0807 0 (916)322・5660


Page Two
December 20′  1990
2. The last paragraph of the resolution needs to state lrhere
the statenents of econonic i-nterests are to be fiIed. 
If the
pl-ace of fi1ing is with the city clerk, then the following
paragraph would suffice: 
"A11 designated enployees shall file
their statenents of economic interests with the city c1erk.
3. In the opening paragraph of Item I, the List of
designated positions, j.t is suggested that the second sentence be
revised to readr 'tThe designated positions listed shal1 dj,sclose
their economic interests in accordance with the corresponding
disclosure categories.,.rr rather than 'rinterests and investnents. tt
4. A11 designated positions have been assigned category 1,
and dependi,ng on the position, categories 2 through 4 may have
been assigned as v/e11. category t however is an aI1 inclus j.ve
category which requires the discLosure of all financial interests
within tne city of west sacramento. For all positions assigned
category 1, the assignment of additional categories is
unnecessary. Any interests required to be disclosed under
categories 2 through 4 is al-ready required to be disclosed under
category 1.
5. There is an asterisk behind the subheading Desiqnated
Position*. This asterisk brings the reader to a footnote which
advises the reader that the term rrDesignated Positionrr includes
any other positions as designated by the city Manager. we have a
concern witfr tnis statement. As it reads it appears that this
gives the city manager the discretion to add any position at any
ti,ne.
covernnent code Section 8731-1 provides that the review and
preparation of codes by loca1 governnent agencies sha}l be carried
out under procedures which guarantee to officers, employees,
members, and consultants of the aqency and to residents of the
jurisdiction adequate notice and a fair opportunity to present
thej,r views. Further, any changes to a code must be apProved by
the city council before those changes become effective (Section
87303). we do not believe the city manager may, at anytime, add
positions to the code without those persons being given rradequate
notice and a fair opportunity to present their views.rr Further,
any arnendments to the code must be approved by the city council.
It is suggested that this asterisk and its footnote be deleted.
6. The list of designated posi-tions includes the city
attorney. Section 87200 of the Act governs the disclosure and
disqual if ication obligations for city attorneys. This position
should be deleted from the conflict of interest code.
7. Your code does not provide for the disclosure of econornic
interests by consultants for the aqency. The definition of a
designated enployee includes consultants. (Section 820L9.) A
consuLtant is any natural person who provides under contract,
information, advice, recomrnendation or counsel to any agency.
(Regulation 18700. )


Page Three
December 20, 1990
The commission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the makj,ng of decisions on behalf of public agencies. Rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-naking capacity, the Cornmission endorses the use of a
specialized disclosure category that provides that the disclosure
r-guired of consuLtants shall be detennined on a case-by-case
laJis Uy the city manager or city attorney. The city manager or
city attorney shall make a deterrnination as to what disclosure, if
any, is required by any particular consultant. A copy of this
consultant disclosure category is enclosed, and should be
incorporated into your code.
8. with the disclosure categories themselves, it j-s
suggested that the monetary thresholds in each be deleted. The
monetary thresholds are set out in the model provisions of the
code (Regulation 18730). Should a legislative amendment increase
or decreise the monetary threshold for reportj-ng interests, the
disclosure category portj,on of your code would be out of date and
in need of amendment. The nonetary threshotds are set out in the
model code as well as the discLosure forms thernselves.
The amount of verbiage in each category could be reduced by
rewriting each category. For instance, category 1 could.read:
'rAIl- investnents and business positions in business entities,
sources of incorne and interests in real property.'l
Category 2, if you continue to use it, is a L j-ttle confusj-ng
in its present format. It appears that you are asking for
investmlnts in business entities which have an interest in real
property in the city or which are of the type to contract with the
Liti. 
any tine you are asking for an i,nvestment interest to be
dis-Iosed, you should also require the corresponding business
positj.ons and incorne from those sources as weI1. category 2
sfrould be re'rrritten to read: rrlnvestments and business positions
in business entities, and sources of income from entities which
have an j.nterest in real property within the cj,ty or entities
which are of the t)rpe to do business with the city.'i 
You will
note that r have deleted the references to "doinq business during
the year previous to the date of disclosure" as welL as to the
reference to entities which rrare likely to do business with the
city. " An investment is reportable if it is doing business or has
done business in a jurisdiction during the tr.ro years prior to the
time any statement is filed (Section a2o34). The reference to
only disclosing entities if the enployees believes is "Iikel-y" to
do business with the City places a judgrnent call on the enpLoyee.
If an enti,ty has a two-year track history of doing bus j,ness with
the city, it should be disclosed, regardless of whether the
employee believes it could do business in the subsequent year,
Category 3 needs to have business position disclosure added
to it and should read: trlnvestments and business positions in
business entitj.es, interests in real property and sources of
incorne subject to the regulatory...rl


category 4 also needs business position disclosure added
would read: rrlnvestments and business positions in business
entities, and sources of j-ncone which engage in land
development. . . tl
f hope this has been helpful to you. If you need further
j.nforrnatj.on or clarification, 
please feel free to give our office
a call- at (915) 322-59ot.
Page Four
December 20′  1990
SH/」ET/jt
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The City of West Sacramento requests FPPC assistance in reviewing and updating its conflict of interest code, including suggested changes or revisions needed to ensure compliance with the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides eight specific areas of concern and recommended revisions to the City's conflict of interest code, including correcting regulatory references, clarifying filing locations for statements of economic interests, revising disclosure category language, removing unnecessary category overlaps, deleting the city manager's unilateral position designation authority, removing the city attorney position, adding consultant disclosure provisions, and eliminating monetary thresholds from category descriptions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with no explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers; question and conclusion information extracted from body text. Document provides detailed itemized feedback rather than a single conclusory statement. Footnotes provide important context regarding informal vs. formal advice and statutory/regulatory references.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The City of West Sacramento requests FPPC assistance in reviewing and updating its conflict of interest code, including suggested changes or revisions needed to ensure compliance with the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides eight specific areas of concern and recommended revisions to the City's conflict of interest code, including correcting regulatory references, clarifying filing locations for statements of economic interests, revising disclosure category language, removing unnecessary category overlaps, deleting the city manager's unilateral position designation authority, removing the city attorney position, adding consultant disclosure provisions, and eliminating monetary thresholds from category descriptions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the City of West Sacramento's request for informal assistance in updating its conflict of interest code by providing detailed feedback on eight substantive areas requiring revision, including regulatory citations, disclosure procedures, position designations, category assignments, and consultant provisions.
